data.name: Monstera deliciosa

image.alt: Large Monstera leaves with characteristic fenestrations

data.fact: The holes and splits in mature leaves (fenestrations) are thought to let wind pass through in the wild, reducing damage during storms in tropical forests.

data.name: Lavandula angustifolia

image.alt: Purple lavender flowers in a field

data.fact: Lavender’s fragrance comes from essential oils in tiny glands on the flowers, leaves, and stems — bees visit it heavily, which helps nearby fruit crops.

data.name: Bambusa vulgaris

image.alt: Tall green bamboo stalks in a grove

data.fact: Bamboo is one of the fastest-growing plants on Earth; some species can grow nearly a meter in a single day under ideal conditions.
